extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI Michał won from the breakaway at Grand Colombier, while POGAČAR attacked in final kilometer gaining 4 seconds on VINGEGAARD with time bonus.
tactical_patterns: UAE controlled breakaway gap at 1:26-1:27 for 50km, preventing team car access to dehydrate breakaway riders strategically.
POGAČAR left stage win attack until final 50 seconds specifically to collect time bonus, gaining 4s on VINGEGAARD.
POWLESS sacrificed stage points intentionally to save energy for stage 14 with 52 KOM points available.
UAE applied pressure before intermediate sprint forcing 19-rider split, then allowed expansion to 20 to control composition.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: positive: survived 137km breakaway despite UAE control strategy designed to dehydrate escapees, sprinted for second Tour stage win
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: tactically sophisticated late attack for time bonus only, now pressuring VINGEGAARD three consecutive mountain stages
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: lost time third consecutive mountain stage, team publicly stating they're waiting for higher Alpine stages to respond
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: neutral: moved to 6th overall with comfortable ride, positioning for final week
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: intelligently conceded KOM points to preserve legs for stage 14 where 52 points available
rider_signals.SHAW James: positive: second place from breakaway behind KWIATKOWSKI, strong climbing against GC team pressure
rider_signals.VAN GILS Maxim: neutral: animated breakaway but caught by POGAČAR attack in finale
